DISCHARGE SUMMARY
Memorial Regional Medical Center — Hospital Medicine
Admission: 2025-08-28
Discharge: 2025-08-31
Attending: P. Okafor, MD

ADMISSION DIAGNOSIS
Community-acquired pneumonia, right upper lobe, with hypoxia.

DISCHARGE DIAGNOSES
1. Community-acquired pneumonia, right upper lobe, resolving.
2. Incidental right upper lobe density on chest x-ray, requires outpatient
   CT chest for further characterization. Differential includes post-
   infectious change versus underlying mass; recommend dedicated imaging
   given smoking history.
3. Hypertension, controlled.
4. COPD without exacerbation during this admission.
5. Former tobacco use (45 pack-years, quit 2014).

HOSPITAL COURSE
Mr. Doe is a 72-year-old gentleman who presented with three days of cough,
fever to 38.7 C, and exertional dyspnea. Initial chest x-ray showed a right
upper lobe opacity with surrounding consolidation. He was admitted, started
on ceftriaxone and azithromycin, and improved over 48 hours. Repeat chest
x-ray prior to discharge showed partial resolution of consolidation but
persistence of a more focal density in the apical segment, prompting
recommendation for outpatient CT.

He was afebrile for 24 hours prior to discharge with oxygen saturations of
95% on room ambulation. Pulmonology follow-up arranged.

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS
- Amoxicillin-clavulanate 875/125 mg PO BID x 5 days to complete course.
- Tiotropium 18 mcg inhaled daily (continue home med).
- Lisinopril 20 mg PO daily (continue home med).
- Atorvastatin 40 mg PO QHS (continue home med).

FOLLOW-UP
- Pulmonology: 2025-09-05 with Dr. Patel.
- CT chest with contrast scheduled outpatient.
- Primary care: within two weeks.

P. Okafor, MD — electronically signed 2025-08-31 11:04
